entry.title: And that's a wrap - SIT Madrid 2025
entry.content:
Last Friday, 21st of March, we restarted the SAP Inside Track "chapter" in Madrid. The day was full of interesting sessions, good food, and interesting networking opportunities.
 

 

Below, are the different talks that were part of the event:
  • 09:30 - Level up your CAP skills by learning how to use the cds repl ( @qmacro  )
  • 10:00 - Knowledge Graphs: From Fundamentals to Implementation with SAP S/4HANA and Enterprise Applications ( @Antonio_leites )
  • 10:30 - How SAP Cloud ALM helps you to implement and operate intelligent cloud and hybrid business solutions ( @maria_teresa_gutierrez )
  • 11:30 - SAP Business AI: Latest Breakthroughs and Future Prospects ( @salva_gimeno )
  • 12:00 - Code10 CI Accelerator - The Integration Suite Addon ( @Antonio_C10IT )
  • 12:30 - Building flexible Event-Driven Architectures with open standards ( @ajmaradiaga  )
  • 13:30 - Building a Personal AI Fitness Coach with SAP BTP, STRAVA, and Ollama GenAI ( @IgorNarodowy  )
  • 14:00 - Mastering Connectivity & Accelerating Integrations with prepackaged integration content ( @rcvblanco @and@sanderkool  )
  • 14:30 - SAP Customer Insights Dashboard ( @Lluis )

 

You weren't able to make it to the in-person event? That's not a problem. The entire day was live streamed on YouTube, meaning that you can watch the recordings of the different sessions.

Sessions from 09:30 - 11:00

Session from 11:30 - 13:00

Sessions from 13:30 - 15:00

Thanks to all our speakers and everyone who joined us in person and online. It was a great day and quite interesting to know other members of the SAP Community in Madrid. I'm already looking forward to the next SIT Madrid ;-).

entry.title: Re: Onboarding New Hires Authentication using SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS)
entry.content:
Hello @Gopinath ,

Fundamentally what I was trying to get at is the real world human impact of requiring Onboardees to login to SF via IAS.

No doubt the IAS login should be configured correctly so there’s no errors when logging in.

However correct configuration doesn’t change the fact that the real world steps that the Onboardees need to take just to login via IAS are *numerous and by definition inherently difficult*.

Absolutely every Onboardee in the world has to somehow know exactly what to do in sequence, just to login to SuccessFactors: Receive email, Activate IAS, download authenticator with their mobile device, scan QR code etc. Failure to complete any step will result in inability to login to SuccessFactors.

 

What I was saying is also the fact that Onboardees are not even employees at the time of doing this login to SF. Technical support may not be readily accessible to help them to even login in the SF. This is a risk factor as they may even give up trying to login to SF.

Of course you or any one in SAP won’t find any difficulty in activating IAS and logging in to SF. After all, you are an expert from SAP.

But here we are talking about countless Onboardees around the world, with different experiences with technology (let alone SAP IAS), different education level, etc, all forced to somehow know the required steps to login to SF via IAS. Also you mention logging in to Gmail etc: Gmail does not require users to download an Authenticator app, scan QR code etc.

Think about the real world human impact on the ground

Best Regards

Ernest Lin

entry.title: Re: ZR35 Rule error
entry.content: For that PERNR and for the Day the error happens (21.03.2025), execute pt60 with the "Display Log" box checked. You should then be able to see exactly in which PCR and after which Operation the error happens. Please post a screen capture of that so that we could help you since pcr ZR35 is a Custom PCR, and the error could happen during the processing of any Operation it contains.

entry.title: Re: Sec 603 automatically move catch-up contributions to...
entry.content: Here is SAP's answer

entry.title: Re: Sec 603 automatically move catch-up contributions to...
entry.content: Updated 25.03.2025 at 08:57 by SAP Agent Info for Customer Proposed Solution: Hello Agnes, Currently SAP does not have a process of automatic plan selection and there are no plans to deliver a functionality with this option. As it is necessary to have different configuration for pos-tax and pre-tax plans in the benefits tables, it is not possible for the same plan to converted from pre-tax to post-tax. That said, as it would be necessary to have two different plans and there can exists multiple plans configured in an environment, currently there is no way to know which of the existing plans available in the system should be used for an employee, preventing an automatically plan selection. Since Product support channel is designated for technical/functional issues with an standard SAP product and this case is related to the request of a new functionality, please consider reaching out to your consulting team directly for further help on this case, since this is a consulting issue. You may refer to the KBA 0000083020 - "What is consulting - What is support? and 0001054121 - The SAP Ecosystem in a Nutshell Best Regards, Gisele Technical Support - SuccessFactors HCM SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Onboarding New Hires Authentication using SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS)
entry.content:
@sunilgr  Exactly! It should be hassle free. But soon SAP is going to sunset this basic SF login for Onboardees.

Imagine the HR teams that will be bombarded by how-to questions from Onboardees.

Worse, Onboardees can just ghost the company due to these technical difficulties and give up joining the company!

As anyone can imagine, first impressions are important. The SF Onboarding product team of all people should fully appreciate this. Any adverse interaction that the new hire or pre-joiner has with the company has an outsized impact on their impression of the company due to the limited interactions they have had with the company thus far.

Companies need to treat pre-joiners with care as pre-joiners by definition have inherent flight-risks or risk of no show. The last thing anyone wants is for the technical hassles of login via IAS to be a contributing factor to their frustrations and decision not to join the company.

entry.title: SuccessFactors Sync SF - Microsoft Entra - Multiple Employment/Contingent
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

we have set up an integration between Successfactors and Microsoft Entra. The sync and writeback are also working for us so far. However, we are having problems with employees with multiple employment, employees who change from a fixed-term to a permanent contract and with contingent workers.

  • In the case of multiple employees, integration “rolls the dice” as to which employment is taken. As a result, it regularly happens that the inactive employment is synced and the account is deactivated in AD.
  • As soon as an employee's termination is deleted due to a permanent contract, the AD receives the value NULL for the expiration date. The value NULL does not overwrite the old value in the AD and must therefore be deleted manually.
  • In the case of contingent workers, we have no termiantion and therefore no termination date. The Work Order End Date can be used as a replacement.

Has anyone already gained experience with the integration and solved these challenges (via one application)? We would be very happy to hear from you.

Best regards
Sascha Grünner

entry.title: Automatic sending of SalesOrder messages from the S/4 HANA Public Cloud to SAP Event Mesh
entry.content:
Hello Community,

When a new SalesOrder message is created or updated in the S/4 HANA Public Cloud, I would like this message to be automatically triggered in the SAP Event Mesh and stored in the salesorder queue of the SAP Event Mash. I have already stored an “event mesh” system with the corresponding host and outbound communication (OAuth 2.0 via clientID and clientsecret from the event mesh) in the S/4 HANA Public Cloud under Communication Systems. I have created a sales order queue directly in the event mesh under the corresponding message client and stored the topic “sap/s4/beh/salesorder/created/v1”. What steps still need to be taken explicitly in the S/4 HANA Public Cloud so that the sales orders are automatically sent to the queue?

In general, I later retrieve the contents of the queue from the cloud integration via an AMQP adapter and then the other SalesOrder information using an OData call.

Thanks in advance and best regards

Michael Seydler

entry.title: Re: SAP Service and Asset Manager Gis Enhancement
entry.content: The default search is not based on geometry but based on description and related fields. You can check the metadata and search for "SearchFields" and modify those and check if that works but we can only search that's a direct property of the entity. So if you are searching for a geometry that's not a direct property of for example: MyWorkOrderHeaders, but a navigation property then it wont work.

at de.hybris.platform.servicelayer.search.FlexibleSearchQuery.addQueryParameters(FlexibleSearchQuery.java:170) ~[coreserver.jar:?]

This blog doesn’t close the loop to explain what happens after onboarding. All Onboardees become employees eventually.

entry.title: Re: Expiry Date Adjustment Upon HU Unpacking in EWM
entry.content:
Hello MahdiNaji,

I had the same requirement couple of years ago in a pharma company.
We search in EWM standard and couldn't find anything. Business also didn't want to create a new batch for the container which was just open.

So we create a Ztable with the HU and a "reduced shelf life" date.

After you have to use adjust some processes:

  1. Calculate the reduced shelf life during the opening process (for us it was sampling, partial picking) & update Z table
  2. Search - Automatically search and sort for the HU with reduced shelf life / normal shelf life (at batch level)
  3. Report to display the Reduced shelf life and block it when expired.

BR,

Emmanuel.

entry.title: Re: Manually Editing the Amount in Settlement Document| S...
entry.content: Normally REV1 is used as a final rebate amount for the entire contract. Technically you can use it at any other table, but this means that REV1 will apply to the specific line items of the settlement document which are matching to the table being used. REV1 is added in the contract manually, you cannot calculate it in standard.

entry.title: Yard Logistics: Self Check-In: Empty Error Message when SAP GUI/Launchpad is set to Language "DE"

I have an issue with the Self Check-In App in Yard Logistics.

Two different system behaviours:

    • Open Self-Check-In App
    • Choose Language German or English in Self Check-In App
    • Self-Check-In works without Error message

 

  • SAP GUI/Fiori Login Language: DE (German)
    • Open Self-Check-In App
    • Choose Language German or English in Self Check-In App
    • Type in Number of Yard Order
    • Go to Next Step
    • Register New Driver
    • Type in Forename and Surname of Driver 
    • Go to Next Step
    • Enjoy this Error Message: 

My guess is, that somewhere a translation or customizing regarding the german language is missing. But I couldn't find anything. Another guess is, that it could be a system setting, because the system language is the problem not the language in the app itself. 

Any ideas for a solution? Or any ideas where I could check settings?

Many greetings, 

Sandra

entry.title: Re: Onboarding New Hires Authentication using SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS)
entry.content:
@Gopinath not true, when I was enabling IAS for Onboardees for a customer back in 2023 after it was released, 2FA steps were required from Onboardees.

entry.title: SAP Business One IAM / Service Layer get logged User
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I need some help understanding how I can retrieve the user code of the logged-in user in my application, which is authenticated via Single Sign-On (SSO).

For clarity, here’s the scenario:

  • The user logs into my application via SSO.

  • The account used for SSO login is linked (in the Control Center) to a SAP user.

  • Sometimes, the SSO username does not exactly match the SAP username (or code), so I need to find a way to retrieve the linked SAP user.

I am able to obtain the logged-in username from my code by reading it from the authentication token (if you know of a call that does the same, even better), but I am unsure how to retrieve the linked SAP user.

I checked the database but didn’t find anything, nor did I find a relevant service layer endpoint.
Of course, I cannot use the "Users" endpoint since I don't have the SAP user details.

I appreciate any useful information in advance.

Thanks!

entry.title: Generative AI-Based Script Optimization
entry.content:
Authors: Marc Eiro, Mitko Kolev, Markus Muenkel, Syed Muhammad Kumail Raza

Product version 6.54 witnessed the first Generative AI feature to SAP Integration Suite, as described in the blog SAP Integration Suite – Generative AI based Integration Flow Generation. 

In product version 6.59 we are adding another Generative AI feature, which is dedicated to optimization of Groovy scripts. This feature is available with SAP Integration Suite, Premium Edition, on specific regions/data centres eu10, eu10-002, eu10-003, eu10-005, us10, us10-002. Roll-out has been performed in calendar week 50.

The enablement of Generative AI features is documented in above blog and product documentation. 

 

Introduction

In our previous blog Inspecting and Understanding Resource Consumption of your Integration Content we explained how to inspect the resource consumption of the integration flows. The Inspect feature (see https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/inspect) enables understanding resource-related issues and helps in mitigation by proposing adjustments in the integration content. This effectively constitutes a feedback channel from the operation phase back into the design phase. An end-to-end flow, from problem detection to problem resolution, was showcased in Michael Ameling's keynote at TechEd 2024. In this example, a memory problem was detected in the Inspect tool, and traced down to a Groovy script in one of the integration flows. The Script Optimization was used to improve memory consumption in that Groovy code, and after redeployment of the integration flow the Inspect feature confirmed that the memory bottleneck was resolved.

The Script Optimization feature is automating the proposals for improvements on resource consumption and compliance with best practices in Groovy scripts. This is achieved by using a LLM (large language model) that is grounded with best practices and design guidelines for integration content.

Following is a step-by-step description of how to use the feature.

 

Generative AI-Based Script Optimization

In order to use the feature, you have to display the integration flow in edit mode within Design view. When you open the Groovy code editor, the "Optimize" button is displayed:

The new "Optimize" button in the Groovy script editor

Clicking the "Optimize" button retrieves suggestions from Generative AI, which are based on best practices and design guidelines. The suggestions are shown in a "AI-Generated Review Report" after a short while:

The AI-Generated Review Report for the Groovy Script

This report consists of three parts:

General assessment
Either this assessment will confirm that the script adheres to guidelines and there would be no further suggestions for improvement. Otherwise it would summarise where the script might need improvement.

Recommended Changes
In this case the recommended changes are listed down, each coming with a title, explanation and a related code snippet.

Improved Script
Finally, a complete script is provided that showcases all of the above recommendations. Let's reveal it by scrolling the report to the bottom:

The proposal for an improved script

The user can choose to adopt individual recommendations, or the complete improved script, by copying the respective sections in the report and pasting in the editor.

This feature is currently provided on the basis of free and fair usage option. Due to this, there will be a certain usage limit per tenant per calendar month for next 6 months. If you exhaust this limit, this will be notified via a message saying "The AI request could not be processed because you have reached your fair usage token quota." when using the "Optimize" button. 

 

Conclusion

We introduced the Generative AI-based "Script Optimization" feature to help our customers optimizing their Groovy scripts for enterprise readiness by streamlining resource consumption and adhering to best practices. This feature facilitates the end-to-end action to insight cycle where issues at runtime are detected in the Inspect feature, traced to the root cause, are understood and fixed, and resolution can be ultimately validated in Inspect tool.

entry.title: Step by Step Implementation of Customer-Defined Events in Business Event Logging
entry.content:
Creating Customer Events for Customer Business Objects 

 
Context:  

In the earlier blog we introduced various scenarios in which business events can be extended and logged via Business Event Logging. In this blog we will explain the steps needed to implement a custom scenario with all its variations. 

 

Environment:  

This feature is available for customers working with the SAP BTP ABAP environment, but it also works for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private, and S/4HANA (On-Premises) editions from release 2022 FPS 2 onwards. 

 

Pre-requisites:  
 

To get started, ensure that you have the following roles:  

 

 

 

You must also create 2 custom roles an example is given below:  

  •  ZBEL_CONFIG using the template SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT to configure Business Event Logging. 
  •  ZBEL_MONITORING using the template SAP_BR_BUSINESS_PROCESS_SPEC to check the event log. 

A role created using the template SAP_BR_DEVELOPER is also needed to create the required ABAP artifacts for this scenario. 

You'll also need the ADT for Eclipse IDE 

 Explanation: For this example, we use a customer RAP Business object with the following structure: 
 

This is a simple RAP Business Object with a Header Node and Item Node and set of attributes corresponding to each node. 

To cover Business Event Logging related scenarios, we have defined two item-related events ItemStatusChanged and ItemQtyChanged and one header level event StatusChanged. All these events transport field change information. 

The equivalent Behaviour definition (BDEF) looks like below  

 

 

Modelling Event Parameter:   

From the BDEF we see that each event has a deep parameter assigned to it. This parameter stands for the event payload format. The deep format is used here, as it is the standard way to transport field changes.  

For the event parameter ZD_ITEMQTYCHANGED for event ItemQtyChanged, the event parameter represents the event payload (the business data beyond the key). The payload is modelled using abstract entity. The abstract entity ZD_ITEMQTYCHANGED is a nested structure with certain fields and a node named as __before (two underscores) of type ZD_ITEMQTYCHANGEDOLD. This is the default way to define field changes in a business event. 

Both the structures have the same fields. The structure ZD_ITEMQTYCHANGED holds the latest values of Quantity and UOM while ZD_ITEMQTYCHANGEDOLD holds the values of Quantity and UOM before the change was executed.  

 

Please note that you are required to maintain the annotation ObjectModel.sapObjectNodeType.name in abstract entities as well as the RAP CDS views. 

 

Below is an illustration of how the event parameters are filled and event raised in the BDEF implementation class. 

 

SAP Object Type and SAP Object Node Type 

 

For each node we need to create the SAP Object Node Type and for header node we create SAP Object Type as well in ADT as shown in below picture. The names should be maintained in CamelCase.  

 

 

 

 

Event Binding: Maintain the event bindings using a customer namespace. 

Business Event Modelling in On-Premise/ Private Cloud Prior 2023 Release: 

In the releases prior to 2023, the event modelling was done in different way as RAP framework didn't allow declaration of a given node event at the respective node. All events had to be declared at the root node level in BDEF. BEL supports logging of the events in this scenario as well, but we need to follow few rules.  

 

 

 

Corresponding BDEF: 

 

For this alternate example we have created SAP Object Types and SAP Object Node Type 

SAP Object Type.           =  ZBusinessObjectHeaderAlt 

SAP Object Node Type = ZBusinessObjectItemAlt 

A careful look at the abstract entity definition you will notice three important distinctions. 

 

 

 

  1. It is mandatory to maintain annotation ObjectModel.sapObjectNodeType.name in all the abstract entity with the correct name. 

     2. For the node level events Event.sapObjectNodeTypeKey annotation need to be maintained with the                  correct names of the Key Names for that node. 

      3. To model the old and new values in abstract entity we need to use event.previousValue.element                       annotation. 

 

  This functionality is available on:   

          ABAP on BTP (Steampunk)  

         SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition 

         SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Private Edition (Release 2022 FPS2 onwards)     

         SAP S/4 HANA On-Premise 2022 FPS2 onwards  

 

Note: In On-Premise and Private Cloud Editions for release 2022, the functionality to create SAP Object Type (SOT) and SAP Object Node Type (SONT) is not available for customer. The customer can explicitly define SOT in the event binding and SONT in the annotations. Care should be taken that the names are in camel case. 

 

 

Once all the above considerations are met in a RAP BO we can use BEL  by a simple configuration. We need to maintain SAP Object Type via the steps given below and select Activate Logging and Log All Data Options. With this we are good to go with logging our custom business events using BEL. 

 

 

 

Execution and Monitoring:  

For the demo purpose I have created an ABAP code to consume the RAP BO using EML thereby triggering these events multiple times. We can visualize these logs using BEL apps. 

 

Display Business Event Log App 

 

 

Customer events can always be identified via the entry in the Producer Namespace Column.  

Detail View of Item Quantity Changed 

The source of the demo example is provided at the below given git repo.

https://github.com/aarsaraf/DemoExample.git

entry.title: Event-Driven Data Integration from SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2 to SAP Datasphere (Part 2 of 2)
entry.content:
In part 1 of this article series, we have motivated the usefulness of event driven data integration for near real-time analytical applications, and built a plain end-to-end scenario between SAP Sales and Service Cloud Version 2 (SSCV2) and SAP Datasphere: 

Event-Driven Data Integration from SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2 to SAP Datasphere (Part 1 of 2) 

In this second part, we will take the scenario that has been built as a basis for more complex integration scenarios, where integrated data entities are referencing each other in different ways:

  • 1 to 0..1 Relationship based on Foreign Key
  • Code Value References - File based Download/Upload
  • Simple Collection Reference - DB Field of Type Array
  • Complex Collection Reference - Use of Relationship Tables

If not done before, the required artefacts - iFlow definition and DDL statements for HANA Cloud table creation can be downloaded from the following GitHub repository:

https://github.com/sapstefan/SSCV2-Datasphere-Integration 

 

1 to 0..1 Relationship based on Foreign Key

We want to enhance the data model by joining the Cases data source to the Individual Customer data source. This can be established with a simple foreign key relationship. The Cases DB table already has an INDIVIDUAL_CUSTOMER_ID field,  so we create another database table for Individual Customers, using the CREATE TABLE statement from the downloaded ind_customers_create_table.sql file.

The table created by this statement should look as follows.

The mapping for Individual Customer has already been deployed with the iFlow. So, create an Individual Customer now in SSCV2: 

This should trigger the Individual Customer event and via the iFlow create an entry in the Individual Cusomers table on Datasphere / HANA Cloud.

Use the Data Builder view of your Datasphere tenant to create a view that joins the Cases data source with the Individual Customers data source in your space. The View Name here is "SSCV2_CASES_VIEW".

There should be a Left Join between the CASES table / data source and the INDIVIDUAL_CUSTOMERS data source / table, which maps the CASES.INDIVIDUAL_CUSTOMER_ID to INDIVIDUAL_CUSTOMERS.ID.

For simple validation, the view can for example be reduced to the following fields through the projection:

  • CASES.DISPLAY_ID
  • CASES.CASE_TYPE
  • CASES.SUBJECT
  • CASES.STATUS
  • INDIVIDUAL_CUSTOMERS.FORMATTED_NAME

Deploy the view. The result should then look as follows, when displaying the data of the view, and after a case has been created for the new customer.

Code Value References - File based Download/Upload

The next scenario of data references looks at code lists, which typically consist of code value / name / description combinations. Let's take the service category hierarchy of SSCV2 as an example. The case object / record keeps the code values, whereas the analytical view should display the code name rather than the code value. The list of categories is supposed to be rather static, and there is no event type in SSCV2 that could be used to integrate changes between SSCV2 and DataSphere. We therefore use a simple CSV file download / upload mechanism, to create a DB table and entries in DataSphere for service categories.

Download the list of service categories from SSCV2 via Settings - "Import and Export Data".

The exported csv file can directly be used in the Datasphere HANA Cloud DB, to create a table and fill in the content, using the "Import Data" function.

Verify table and content creation in the database explorer:

Now, the category code values of the Case data source can be joined with the Categories data source. Again choose a left join to map the code values to each other, and add additional fields to the view from the Categories data source - e.g. L1 Category Name, L2 Category Name.

The result view and content should look like this.

 

Simple Collection Reference - DB Field of Type Array

The next use case looks at referencing collections of related entities. For example, a single Case in SSCV2 can be created for multiple assigned Registered Products. Simple means that a reference doesn't carry any further attributes. 

If not done yet, let's first create the REGISTERED_PRODUCTS table via DDL statement from the dowloaded file registered_products_create_table.sql.

In this scenario, collection references can be managed by adding a field of type Array to the CASES table in DataSphere, by making the following addition to the DDL statement for table creation in DataSphere HANA Cloud. 

A drawback of this approach is that a field of type array is not directly available for view creation in the Data Builder. We therefore have to create a view on DB level first, that joins the CASES table and the REGISTERED_PRODUCTS table, using the ARRAY field. An example DDL statement is again available for download from the GitHub repository as file cases_rp_create_view.sql. This example creates a simple DB view that only provides CASE_ID and REGISTERED_PRODUCT_ID, to link the two entities.

Since the iFlow as deployed in article part 1 already contains the mapping of Registered Products, the creation or change of Registered Products in SSCV2 should lead to data replication into SAP Datasphere HANA Cloud, REGISTERED_PRODUCT table. For Cases with assigned Registered Products, the CASES_RP_VIEW should then provide the corresponding relationship entries.

This DB level view can now be used in the Data Builder (after import), to join additional fields from the Registered Products per Case. If a Case has more than one Registered Product assigned, the resulting analytical view will have multiple entries, as here for the example of Case ID 151.

Detailed view of this Case in SSCV2:

 

Complex Collection Reference - Use of Relationship Tables

In the above screenshot of a case with 2 assigned Registered Products, one of the Registered Products (43) is flagged as the "main" Registered Product. If this information is supposed to be transferred to Datasphere, i.e. an additional attribute at the relationship (isMain), then the apporach of DB table fields of type Array doesn't work anymore. Instead, a separate relationship table will be required, to represent the relationship between Case and Registered Product.

Let's create such a relationship table with additional attribute, with the DDL statement provided in file cases_registered_products_create_table.sql.

Data integration into this relationship table raises an additional challenge. The iFlow needs to be able to transfer a deletion of entries in addition to inserts and updates.

A possible approach could be to introduce a lookup of existing records here per Case, and then send a deletion via the JDBC adapter for all entries that haven't been sent again with the current event. 

A less complex approach (in terms of API call orchestration in the iFlow) is provided here, which is to delete all relationship entries per Case, and then re-create all entries again based on the event sent from SSCV2. An example JDBC XML, to combine DELETE and UPSERTs would look as follows.

 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<root>
    <Delete>
        <dbTableName action="DELETE">
            <table>CASES_REGISTERED_PRODUCTS</table>
            <key>
                <CASE_ID>3a84ad79-a715-11ef-b33d-839ffb830493</CASE_ID>
            </key>
        </dbTableName>
    </Delete>    
    <Upsert>
        <dbTableName action="UPDATE_INSERT">
            <table>CASES_REGISTERED_PRODUCTS</table>
            <key>
                <CASE_ID>3a84ad79-a715-11ef-b33d-839ffb830493</CASE_ID>
                <REGISTERED_PRODUCT_ID>1a9f5be0-cd45-454b-9c80-ce4af9c32ec5</REGISTERED_PRODUCT_ID>
            </key>
            <access>
                <CASE_ID>3a84ad79-a715-11ef-b33d-839ffb830493</CASE_ID>
                <REGISTERED_PRODUCT_ID>1a9f5be0-cd45-454b-9c80-ce4af9c32ec5</REGISTERED_PRODUCT_ID>
                <IS_MAIN>false</IS_MAIN>
            </access>
        </dbTableName>
    </Upsert>
    <Upsert>
        <dbTableName action="UPDATE_INSERT">
            <table>CASES_REGISTERED_PRODUCTS</table>
            <key>
                <CASE_ID>3a84ad79-a715-11ef-b33d-839ffb830493</CASE_ID>
                <REGISTERED_PRODUCT_ID>447c06c7-8afd-4f3b-b3a5-f385244dd977</REGISTERED_PRODUCT_ID>
            </key>
            <access>
                <CASE_ID>3a84ad79-a715-11ef-b33d-839ffb830493</CASE_ID>
                <REGISTERED_PRODUCT_ID>447c06c7-8afd-4f3b-b3a5-f385244dd977</REGISTERED_PRODUCT_ID>
                <IS_MAIN>true</IS_MAIN>
            </access>
        </dbTableName>
    </Upsert>
</root>

 

The XML combines three statements in one message:

  • DELETE of all CASES_REGISTERED_PRODUCT entries for the given Case ID
  • UPSERT of all entries into the same table, as transferred by the SSCV2 event
  • UPSERT of the CASES entry for the Case that has triggered the event from SSCV2.

The respective part of the Case message mapping of the attached iFlow looks as follows. 

Processing of these multiple statements in one call as a single transaction requires the Batch Mode to be active on the JDBC sender adapter in the iFlow.

With the provided example iFlow, both techniques are tested - field of type Array and relationship table. Entries in the relationship table are transferred as follows, note that the additional information about "IS_MAIN" is provided.

And the table can be used in the analytical view, again by joining the relationship table data source with the CASES data source.

entry.title: Manufacturer Part Numbers in Purchase Orders.
entry.content:
Manufacturer Part Number (MPN) in Purchase Orders

 

Manufacturer Part number is a unique identification code assigned by a manufacturer to identify specific product or a component. It is primarily used for tracking the inventory and ordering.

While placing the order user can have a provision to select a particular manufacturer for the material.

A vendor X supplies Bolts manufactured by two different manufacturers and user wishes to order Bolts from the Vendor X but Vendor X to supply the materials only from Manufacturer B.

  • Bolt A from Manufacturer A
  • Bolt B from Manufacturer B

Steps for configuration in SAP.

Step 1: Manufacturer Part Number indicator is to be set in customizing.

SPRO-> Logistics – General -> Material Master -> Basic Settings -> Make Global Settings

Tcode: OTM0

Step2: Define Manufacturer Part Profile

SPRO-> Materials Management-> Purchasing-> Material Master-> Define Manufacturer Part Profile

  • Order Text for MPN Material – If you set this indicator, the order text for the MPN material will be adopted in the purchase order also If you do not set the indicator, the order text for the firm's own material will be adopted.

 

  • MPN mandatory - If you set this indicator, the MPN material must be specified in the purchase order  and in the scheduling agreement instead of the material number.

 

  • Change in MPN - If you set this indicator, changes to the MPN material are allowed during the procurement process (e.g. in requisitions, when creating a purchase order by referencing a requisition, when working with supplier confirmations, and at the time of goods receipt).

 

  • Info Records for MPN – If you set this indicator, info records can only be created for MPN materials, NOT for your firm's own materials. This enables you to store different prices for the MPN materials.

 

Step 3: Create Material Master - In the material creation process, enter the Manufacturer Part Profile created in the earlier step.

Step 4: Creation of Manufacturer Part Numbers for the above material.

Create a new material type referring to the HERS Standard Material type.

Select Purchasing View:

Step 5: BP’s to be created in the system –

Manufacturer A created as a BP - 

Manufacturer B created as a BP - 

Step 6: Create Manufacturer Parts.

Manufacturer Material A –

Manufacturer Material B

Step 7: Creation of Info Records for the Manufacturer Part Number & the manufacturer.

Step 8: Create Purchase Requisition.

User gets a choice to select the manufacturer in the line item Sources of Supply. 

Select the Source of Supply in the line item.

PR created with user preferred MPN.

Step 9: PO Creation with main vendor with manufacturer part number for the main material.

Hence the for a material, multiple manufacturer parts can be maintained and orders can be placed to the main vendor.

Regards,

Sagnik.

entry.title: Customer Vendor Integration (CVI) during S4 HANA Conversion (Brown Field Implementation)
entry.content:
Customer Vendor Integration (CVI) during S4 HANA Conversion

In SAP S/4HANA, the concept of integrating customer and vendor master data into a single entity called the Business Partner is introduced. This allows the same person or company to act as both a customer and a vendor, reducing the complexity of data management. The Business Partner (BP) concept is central to CVI. During S4 HANA Conversion, SAP offers tools and transactions to ensure the master data consistency check along with configuration, monitoring, and execution of the CVI conversion.

Here, Steps 1-4 has to be done in ECC system as pre-requisites and Step 5 has to be done once SAP system is converted to S4 HANA. In this blog, we are focusing on Vendor Integration to Business Partner specifically from MM side. However, the source document attached at the end of the blog covers Customer Master Data Integration as well along with various error troubleshooting mechanisms.

Step 1 : Master Data Consistency Check

The Master Data Consistency Check via CVI_PRECHK transaction is a pre-conversion process in SAP's Master Data Governance (MDG) system that allows users to validate the consistency of customer or vendor master data records. This tool checks whether required fields are populated, ensuring there are no duplicate entries and ensuring that the data adheres to business rules. This allows users to take corrective actions before the CVI run in conversion projects.

1.1In transaction CVI_PRECHK, you need to select Customer or Vendor and enter Server group and Description and click execute.

1.2 Once the job is completed, you can see the logs successfully.

1.3 You can re-run the job by clicking on the red wheel icon and you can open the results of the job log by clicking on the Run ID number. Check and clear all the errors and re-run the job until the error becomes 0.

Step 2: Check Customizing Configurations

In SAP, CVI (Customer-Vendor Integration) customizing checks are essential to ensure that the Customer-Vendor Integration process is properly configured and working as expected. Here are the steps as follows:

2.1 Activate Creation of Post Processing Orders 

IMG -> Cross-Application Components -> General Application Functions -> Post processing Office -> Business Processes -> Activate Creation of Post processing Orders .

Activate Creation of Postprocessing Orders for component AP-MD and Business Processes CVI_01 (Customer > Business Partner) , CVI_02 (Vendor > Business Partner), CVI_03 (Business Partner > Customer) and CVI_04 (Vendor > Business Partner). Mark the checkbox to activate the CVI_01 and 02 entries ONLY before conversion.

2.2 Activate PPO requests for Platform Object 

IMG -> Cross-Application Components ->Master Data Synchronization -> Synchronization Control -> Synchronization Control -> Activate PPO Requests for Platform Objects in the Dialog 

Create a new entry for the synchronization object BP (Business Partner) and mark the checkbox “PPO Active”.

2.3 Activate Synchronization Options 

IMG -> Cross-Application Components -> Master Data Synchronization -> Synchronization Control -> Synchronization Control -> Synchronization Options 

For Business Partner, Customer and Vendor the following entries must exist.

IMG -> Cross-Application Components -> Master Data Synchronization -> Synchronization Control -> Synchronization Control -> Activate Synchronization Options.

Insert new entries for

  • Source Object Customer and Target Object BP (Activate the Checkbox)
  • Source Object Vendor and Target Object BP (Activate the Checkbox)
  • Source Object BP and Target Object Customer
  • Source Object BP and Target Object Vendor

Synchronization options can be maintained with transaction SM30, view MDSV_CTRL_OPT. The following entries must exist:

2.4 Define Number Ranges for Vendor Master 

IMG -> Logistics - General -> Business Partner -> Vendors -> Control -> Define Number Ranges for Vendor Master Records -> Intervals 

2.5 Assign Number Ranges to Account Groups (Vendor) 

IMG -> Logistics - General -> Business Partner -> Vendors -> Control -> Define Number Ranges for Vendor Master Records -> Intervals 

2.6 Define Number Ranges for Business Partner 

IMG -> Cross-Application Components -> SAP Business Partner -> Business Partner -> Basic Settings -> Number Ranges and Groupings -> Define Number Ranges . Here, External Indicator is set.

2.7 Define Groupings and Assign Number Ranges 

IMG -> Cross-Application Components -> SAP Business Partner -> Business Partner -> Basic Settings -> Number Ranges and Groupings -> Define Groupings and Assign Number Ranges 

2.8 Define Number Ranges (for Relationships) 

IMG -> Cross-Application Components -> SAP Business Partner -> Business Partner Relationships -> Basic Settings -> Define Number Ranges 

2.9 Vendor to BP 

IMG -> Cross-Application Components -> Master Data Synchronization -> Customer/Vendor Integration -> Business Partner Settings -> Settings for Vendor Integration -> Define BP Role for Direction Vendor to BP 

2.10 Number Assignment (Vendor to BP) 

IMG: Cross-Application Components -> Master Data Synchronization -> Customer/Vendor Integration -> Business Partner Settings -> Settings for Vendor Integration -> Field Assignment for Vendor Integration -> Assign Keys -> Define Number Assignment for Direction Vendor to BP. Maintain the Vendor Account Group and BP Grouping and the same number indicator is set.

Note: In some cases, the customer and vendor account group has overlapping number ranges and hence maintaining the same number ranges in BP for both grouping will not be possible. In those cases, BP number will vary from the vendor or customer number range. As a result, from step 2.6, you cannot maintain external indicator in BP and from 2.10 you cannot maintain same number indicator as well.

2.11 Assign Contact Person

Although this step is covered by SD consultant in customer master section and this setup is not maintained separately for vendor contact persons, it is mandatory to know the configuration for MM consultant as well.

IMG -> Cross-Application Components -> Master Data Synchronization -> Customer/Vendor Integration -> Business Partner Settings -> Settings for Customer Integration ->Field Assignment for Customer Integration - > Assign Attributes -> Contact Person -> Activate Assignment of Contact Persons

IMG -> Cross-Application Components -> Master Data Synchronization -> Customer/Vendor Integration -> Business Partner Settings -> Settings for Customer Integration -> Field Assignment for Customer Integration -> Assign Attributes -> Contact Person -> Assign Department Numbers for Contact Persons

IMG -> Cross-Application Components -> Master Data Synchronization -> Customer/Vendor Integration -> Business Partner Settings -> Settings for Customer Integration -> Field Assignment for Customer Integration -> Assign Attributes -> Contact Person -> Assign Functions of Contact Person

IMG -> Cross-Application Components -> Master Data Synchronization -> Customer/Vendor Integration -> Business Partner Settings -> Settings for Customer Integration -> Field Assignment for Customer Integration -> Assign Attributes -> Contact Person -> Assign Authority of Contact Person

IMG -> Cross-Application Components -> Master Data Synchronization -> Customer/Vendor Integration -> Business Partner Settings -> Settings for Customer Integration -> Field Assignment for Customer Integration -> Assign Attributes -> Contact Person -> Assign VIP Indicator for Contact Person

2.12 Assign Industries 

IMG -> Cross-Application Components -> Master Data Synchronization -> Customer/Vendor Integration -> Business Partner Settings -> Settings for Vendor Integration -> Field Assignment for Vendor Integration -> Assign Attributes -> Assign Industries 

IMG -> Cross-Application Components -> SAP Business Partner -> Business Partner -> Organizations -> Maintain Industry Systems and Industries 

Step 3: Synchronization Process

In SAP, the Synchronization Cockpit for Customer-Vendor Integration (CVI) is a tool used to monitor and manage the synchronization or conversion process from traditional customer/vendor master data to the Business Partner (BP) data in the SAP S/4HANA system.

Using the synchronization cockpit, you can carry out all the steps for master data synchronization in an SAP system, especially:

  • Selection, preparation and starting of synchronization runs.
  • Monitoring of synchronization runs
  • Postprocessing of synchronization errors

To start the Synchronization Cockpit, you can call transaction MDS_LOAD_COCKPIT.

3.1 For vendor to BP conversion, select the synchronisation process in the left-hand corner. You can enter the selection criteria such as vendor account group or supplier number and click execute.

Note: Check if the synchronisation program has synchronisation scope as Vendor with Contact Person before proceeding. If the synchronisation scope is vendor without contact person, check the SAP source document to activate business functions or implement SAP notes based on your ECC and SP versions.

3.2 In the Monitor Tab, you can see the status of the job logs for the synchronization process. You should also activate the queue as shown in the image below.

3.3 You can view the error logs by selecting the line item and click on call PPO icon.

3.4 Analyse and correct the errors and re-run the synchronisation process.

You can see the entries in tables such as CVI_VEND_LINK , CVI_VEND_CT_LINK and BUT000 after successful synchronization.

Step 4: Completeness Check:

A Completeness Check in the context of Customer-Vendor Integration (CVI) in SAP refers to verifying that all necessary data required for a customer or vendor to be successfully synchronized into a Business Partner (BP) is available and accurate. This ensures that there are no missing or incomplete pieces of data that could result in issues during the synchronization process from traditional customer/vendor records to the new Business Partner model in SAP S/4HANA.

You can use the transaction CVI_COMPL_CHK for this purpose. Here, you can see the vendor and vendor contact persons that yet to be synchronised successfully. If the transaction is empty, then the synchronisation process is successful, and your work is complete.

Step 5: Post Conversion Process

After completing the Customer-Vendor Integration (CVI) conversion in SAP S/4HANA, there are several post-conversion steps that need to be taken.

5.1 Activate Creation of Post Processing Orders (Direction BP to Customer/Vendor) 

IMG -> Cross-Application Components -> General Application Functions -> Post processing Office -> Business Processes -> Activate Creation of Post processing Orders 

Activate Creation of Postprocessing Orders for component AP-MD and Business Processes CVI_03 (Business Partner > Customer) and CVI_04 (Business Partner > Vendor). Mark the checkbox to activate the entries. 

5.2 Activate Synchronization Options (Direction BP to Vendor) 

IMG -> Cross-Application Components -> Master Data Synchronization -> Synchronization Control -> Synchronization Control -> Activate Synchronization Options  

If not already done in preparation phase, insert new entries for Source Object BP and Target Object Vendor Activate the entry by marking the activation indicator checkbox. 

5.3 Set BP Role Category for Direction BP to Vendor 

IMG -> Cross-Application Components -> Master Data Synchronization -> Customer/Vendor Integration -> Business Partner Settings -> Settings for Vendor Integration -> Set BP Role Category for Direction BP to Vendor 

5.4 Number assignment (CVI-Target: Vendor) 

Suppose previously in ECC system before S4 HANA conversion, for BP grouping IMPO, we have maintained From and To range as 0000200000 and 0000299999 and NR Status is maintained as empty and along with External indicator is set.

After successful S4 HANA conversion, we need to maintain the To number and NR Status with the last record number of the BP account group and remove external indicator. Then, we need to create a new Number Range Interval with From and To range with immediate number from previous NR status and NO external indicator is set. Please check below images to understand thoroughly.

Changes to Old BP Number Range:

Creation and Assignment of New BP Number Range:

Using the below path, then assign the new number range to the BP grouping.

IMG -> Cross-Application Components -> SAP Business Partner -> Business Partner -> Basic Settings -> Number Ranges and Groupings -> Define Groupings and Assign Number Ranges 

IMG -> Cross-Application Components -> Master Data Synchronization -> Customer/Vendor Integration -> Business Partner Settings -> Settings for Vendor Integration -> Field Assignment for Vendor Integration -> Assign Keys -> Define Number Assignment for Direction BP to Vendor 

Set the Vendor Number range to external and Business Partner Number range as internal to ensure that newly created Vendor will have the same numbering as the corresponding Business Partner. Set the flag “Same number”. 

Again, in cases where vendor account group and BP grouping number range is not the same (in cases of overlap as mentioned in step 2.10), the same number indicator cannot be set and external indicator cannot be set in vendor number range.

After the step 5 is completed, you can create Business Partner with General, Customer and Vendor Role.

Source: https://support.sap.com/content/dam/SAAP/SAP_Activate/S4H.0781%20SAP%20S4HANA%20Cookbook%20Customer%20Vendor%20Integration.pdf

entry.title: Stock Load Process for Handling Unit Managed locations
entry.content:
Process : -

  1. Mock Stock Load : -

Focus on Mock stock load in a pre-prod system as part of mock cutover. It is a very important activity. Do not ignore this. It should try to mock exactly what you are going to do during go-live starting from file extraction from current legacy system. Share the learnings with client so that process or method can be improved for actual stock upload in production system. Some of the learnings which we shared with client to improve extract file based on mock cutover were: -

  1. Unique HU must be there in file. No duplicate HU.
  2. Thumb rule is that HU should be loaded in HU managed Sloc. But If you need HU number in a Non-HU Sloc like rework location for scanning, pls provide that.
  3. Each Stock in a HU managed Sloc must have a HU number. Without HU number, stock cannot exist in HU managed Sloc.
  4. Quantity & Value should be in UoM based on which UoM it is palletized.
  5. One Production Date should be there for a batch. Multiple production date for same batch is not allowed.
  6. A value must be provided for each record in file from FI perspective.

 

 

  1. Stock load method in SAP for HU stock : -

 

Pre-work before stock load –

 

  • There should be a cross-reference file to map Legacy plant/sloc to SAP Plant/Sloc.
  • SAP material mapping with legacy material. We used old material number field material master to capture legacy material. It might not work if you are merging multiple legacy materials to one SAP material. 
  • Create new packaging material type and new packaging material with material type VERP to support stock load. Create and assign external HU number ranges to packaging materials as per current legacy handling unit numbers.

 

 

                Actual Stock load in SAP: -

 

We have used LSMW to load stock. This blog is not about which tool is used and why was it used.  This is more on process and method followed for stock load. Although I must say that LSMW worked well for us.

 

  1. First step is to load stock at IM level using 561 movement type in PS01 Sloc which is partner sloc and Non-HU managed.
  2. Load Hus for the material/batches in HU02 in partner sloc PS01 with legacy handling units provided using LSMW that is created for HU02.
  3. Move Hus to their HU storage location using HUMO.  If you move Hus using HUMO, it will not create outbound delivery in background. Hence, it is easier process.

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Change the stock type from unrestricted to either blocked OR quality afterwards based on the stock load file. If you are going to move to Quality, please make sure to deactivate inspection type ‘08’ for the material. Unless you do it, it will unnecessarily create an inspection lot with the stock movement from unrestricted to quality.

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Stock reconciliation: -

 

First & foremost objective is that you should be able to reconcile each and every penny of stock. Unless reconciliation is done properly, there will be lot of issue post go-live and in general, you should not get a green signal for go-live.  In case of your plant & storage location is handling unit managed, you will receive two files from two different business function of client.

 

  • Finance controller from client will send a file at Inventory Managed level. That file will have material/plant/storage location/stock category/Batch/Production Date/Quantity/Unit of Measure.
  • Logistics Lead from client will send a file at HU level. That file will have stock of material/plant/storage location at HU level. This file will also contain pallet type and bin location.
  • Before stock is loaded, reconciliation should be done between both the files (IM and HU). Excel skill is required to do subtotal in this case. In case of quantity mismatch between both the files, an email should be sent to both the parties at client site to check the anomaly. Reconciliation file should be sent in the email. Meanwhile, stock should be loaded parallely for which quantity matched in both the files. For stock mismatch, action should be taken based on client feedback.

 Please feel free to leave comments and feedback. This article will help you in process of LE Handling Unit Management stock load in MM Inventory Management locations , LE Warehouse Management locations. Readers can find Q&A  and post questions in SAP S4 HANA OR MM Inventory Management community.

entry.title: Re: Posting Period Locked
entry.content: If you are working in S4 HANA, you can open the posting period for the required business transaction type with Fiori App Manage Posting Periods - Cost Accounting. Select your company code checkbox and click on open button

entry.title: Re: T5UGB configuration logic.
entry.content:
First, read

  • KBA 2619613 - How to incorporate garnishment state authorities changes in the percentage rule 
  • Tax Law Changes | Minnesota Department of Revenue 

Then update relevant Customizing tables (read documentation from SPRO : SM30 -> Customizing -> Documentation)

  • V_T5UGA: Special Rule for calculation of the Non-Exempt Amount
  • V_T5UGB: Calculation Scheme for Special Rule
  • V_T5UG3: Model for the Calculation of the Non-exempt Amount
  • V_T5UG4: Rules for Calculation of the Non-Exempt Amount

entry.title: Re: IDOC metadata change on SAP backend - Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi Ramu,

Should we ask SAP backend colleagues to share XSD (or) WSDL?

--> Yes ask for XSD.

Which is better practice

----> Asking for Source XSD is best practice

if they share XSD/WSDL then should we also the share the same with receiver BTP CI system contact?

-----> Yes, this case you are the source for next BTP CI system.

Regards,
